mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 6 Series 635d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 6 Series 635d engine (M57TU2) typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. An injector replacement costs £300–£800 per injector. The M57TU2 is a premium diesel requiring specialist expertise.

The M57TU2 is generally robust and won 'Engine of the Year' in 2007. However, piezo injectors can fail on higher-mileage examples, and DPF and EGR systems require monitoring — especially on cars used for short journeys. Regular maintenance and long journeys are essential. With proper care, these engines can cover 200,000+ miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 6 Series 635d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Piezo injector failure (rough idle, misfire, £300–£800 per injector), DPF blockage (warning light, reduced power, £400–£1,200), EGR cooler failure (coolant loss, white smoke, £500–£1,200), and swirl flap failure (loss of power, £500–£1,200). The ZF 6HP28 gearbox also requires regular servicing.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 6 Series 635d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 6 Series 635d values range from £5,000–£15,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £3,500–£5,500 reconditioned engine represents 25–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 6 Series 635d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 635d uses a single engine code: M57TU2 (also referred to as M57D30TU2 or M57N2). This is BMW's 3.0-litre twin-turbo diesel, producing 286bhp. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the injector issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — injectors can be exchanged or replaced (£300–£800 per injector). Symptoms include rough idle, misfire, and poor acceleration. New injectors must be coded to the DDE (engine ECU). Replacing all six together is recommended as they degrade at similar rates. At the first sign of rough idle, book an inspection — ignored injector problems can lead to cylinder wash and engine damage.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the E63/E64 635d M57TU2 generation — a single-generation engine. Reconditioned units are available from around £2,650 upwards. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 6 Series 635d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £120–£170/hr (London/SE dealer). The 6 Series engine bay provides reasonable access, but the twin-turbo setup adds complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 6 Series 635d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace all six together for reliability
Forced regen or cleaning — worth it on a well-maintained car
Replacement often £3,500–£5,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 6 Series 635d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* Documented injector history — replacements or coding records
* DPF and EGR system checked — no warning lights or coolant loss
* ZF 6HP28 gearbox service history (critical for longevity)
avoidIf: * Any evidence of DPF or EGR issues — warning lights, reduced power
* Rough idle or misfire — injector problems are expensive
* Short-journey-only history — DPF issues are almost certain
* Poor service history — the M57 is unforgiving of extended oil changes
* Gearbox faults — the ZF 6HP can be expensive to rebuild

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 635d is a remarkable grand tourer — 286bhp, 580Nm and effortless refinement. The M57TU2 is a powerful and characterful engine that won "Engine of the Year" for good reason. It's generally robust, but injectors, DPF and EGR systems demand attention. A well-maintained example with documented history and regular long journeys is a genuine classic in the making. Buy on history, maintain the fuel and emissions systems, and the 635d will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 6 Series 635d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 6 Series 635d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rough running; M57TU2 should start cleanly
* Idle Quality — Any misfire, lumpiness, or vibration? (injector warning)
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential; check for BMW LL-04 spec
* DPF History — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* EGR System — Any coolant loss or white smoke? EGR cooler failure is common
* Injector Performance — Check for smooth acceleration; injector issues are common on high-mileage cars
* Gearbox Service — ZF 6HP28 requires regular servicing; check history
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? DPF, EGR and injector codes are common
